sections.title: Imitation learning is easy if the robot can assign semantic meaning to the demonstration.

blocks.text: A single demonstration is broken up into sub-actions with semantic labels. These semantic labels allow the system to apply different policies to different parts of the task.

blocks.caption: The demonstration is translated into a semantic representation of trajectories drawn on a plane. The semantic meaning allow the robot to robustly replay the task.

items: A single demonstration is often sufficient.
Once the system understands the underlying interactions, it can replay the task more robustly.
Replay is assembled from semantic meaning.
